critical_fixes_required: Race condition in b2c auto-sync (updateMany without transactionality)
Missing rate limit verification in suspension-status POST
Raw SQL injection vectors in b2c queries
Inadequate error handling in multiple endpoints

P0_BLOCKING_CRITICAL.issue: Auto-sync updates Reservation + AffiliateSale in separate updateMany calls without transaction. Two concurrent requests can create inconsistent state.
P0_BLOCKING_CRITICAL.fix: Wrap both updateMany in prisma.$transaction([...]) to ensure atomicity. If one fails, entire auto-sync rolls back.
P0_BLOCKING_CRITICAL.impact: Data corruption: Reservation marked confirmed but Sale stuck in PENDING

P0_BLOCKING_CRITICAL.issue: Raw SQL template literals use parameter interpolation. Although using $orgId directly, complex WHERE clause makes it vulnerable if input validation is bypassed.
P0_BLOCKING_CRITICAL.fix: Validate orgId is UUID format before use. Better: use Prisma aggregate instead of raw SQL for this simple count.
P0_BLOCKING_CRITICAL.impact: Potential for query manipulation if orgId validation is removed elsewhere

P0_BLOCKING_CRITICAL.category: Missing Rate Limit on State Mutation
P0_BLOCKING_CRITICAL.issue: POST /suspension-status (appeals) has no rate limit. User can spam appeals, filling database.
P0_BLOCKING_CRITICAL.fix: Add checkRateLimit(userId, maxRequests=3, windowMs=24h). Log each appeal attempt.
P0_BLOCKING_CRITICAL.impact: DoS via spam appeals; appeals table inflation; no audit trail

P0_BLOCKING_CRITICAL.issue: Line 31 checks isAdmin for orgFilter, but landings (line 38) use separate landingOrgFilter. Admin=true bypasses landing org scope — potential data leak.
P0_BLOCKING_CRITICAL.fix: Clarify: does GLOBAL_ADMIN (isAdmin=true) intentionally see ALL organizations' landings? If yes, document. If no, fix filter consistency.
P0_BLOCKING_CRITICAL.impact: Admin bypass: admin users see all landing registrations regardless of org assignment

P0_BLOCKING_CRITICAL.issue: Group ownership checked only by groupId + organizationId. But SMS opt-out query (line 64) fetches ALL SmsOptOut records globally without organization filter.
P0_BLOCKING_CRITICAL.fix: Add organizationId filter: where: { organizationId: orgId }. Otherwise, partner A blocks partner B's opt-outs.
P0_BLOCKING_CRITICAL.impact: Cross-organization data leak: partner can see which phones are opted-out by other orgs

P0_BLOCKING_CRITICAL.issue: Share check uses OR: "sharedToOrgId = orgId OR isGlobal = true". If isGlobal=true set on wrong page, any org can clone. No owner verification before clone.
P0_BLOCKING_CRITICAL.fix: Add additional check: original.organizationId !== orgId (prevent self-clone attacks). Validate share.isActive or similar flag.
P0_BLOCKING_CRITICAL.impact: Users clone shared pages without intent; global=true flag leaks to all orgs

P0_BLOCKING_CRITICAL.category: Trusting User Input in External API
P0_BLOCKING_CRITICAL.issue: category parameter passed directly to syncDriveFolder() without format validation. If syncDriveFolder uses it in file path construction, directory traversal risk.
P0_BLOCKING_CRITICAL.fix: Whitelist category: const ALLOWED_CATEGORIES = ['banner', 'logo', 'image']; if (!ALLOWED_CATEGORIES.includes(category.toLowerCase())) { throw ... }
P0_BLOCKING_CRITICAL.impact: Path traversal in Drive sync; attacker could access unintended Drive folders

P1_HIGH_PRIORITY.issue: Auto-sync queries Reservation (100 rows) then separately fetches affiliateSaleId for each — implicit N+1 if not using batching properly.
P1_HIGH_PRIORITY.fix: Already batched by ID array — acceptable. But add pagination if autoConfirmIds > 10k.
P1_HIGH_PRIORITY.impact: Performance: for 1000 autoConfirmIds, 2 extra queries run (findMany + updateMany per type)

P1_HIGH_PRIORITY.issue: Auto-sync error caught but only logged, no user feedback. If sync silently fails, user won't know finalConfirmStatus wasn't updated.
P1_HIGH_PRIORITY.fix: Return partial success: { ok: true, autoSyncAttempted: true, autoSyncFailed: 1, ... }. Allow client to retry.
P1_HIGH_PRIORITY.impact: Silent failures: dashboard shows incomplete data; admin unaware of auto-sync issues

P1_HIGH_PRIORITY.issue: Catch block returns generic '서버 오류' with no context. Difficult to debug.
P1_HIGH_PRIORITY.fix: Log error and return more specific message: if (error instanceof PrismaClientKnownRequestError) { ... }
P1_HIGH_PRIORITY.impact: Poor debugging: can't distinguish DB errors, validation errors, or system failures

P1_HIGH_PRIORITY.issue: Rate limit key (line 103) uses ctx.userId || '' as fallback. If userId is undefined, all anonymous users share same rate limit bucket.
P1_HIGH_PRIORITY.fix: Throw error if userId missing: if (!ctx.userId) throw new Error('USER_ID_REQUIRED');
P1_HIGH_PRIORITY.impact: Rate limit bypass: multiple users under same '' key can collectively send >5 blasts/day

P1_HIGH_PRIORITY.issue: contactGroupMember query takes MAX_RECIPIENTS+1 but filters by opt-out after. If 150 members with 50 opted out, returns only 100 actual targets instead of 200.
P1_HIGH_PRIORITY.fix: Fetch more records upfront to account for opt-outs: take: Math.ceil((MAX_RECIPIENTS) * 1.2)
P1_HIGH_PRIORITY.impact: Undersend: not enough opted-in members targeted; data skew in blast reporting

P1_HIGH_PRIORITY.issue: slug conflict check (findFirst) then create (create). Between check and insert, another clone can create same slug.
P1_HIGH_PRIORITY.fix: Use UNIQUE constraint in DB + try-catch with retry. Or: include timestamp in initial slug.
P1_HIGH_PRIORITY.impact: Duplicate slug: second clone fails with UNIQUE constraint; poor UX

P1_HIGH_PRIORITY.issue: recentLeads maps phone with partial masking but still exposes first 3 digits + last 4. If combined with name, PII re-identifiable.
P1_HIGH_PRIORITY.fix: Mask to: '01*-****-****' (static prefix). Or: require explicit admin intent to unmask.
P1_HIGH_PRIORITY.impact: Privacy: partial PII exposure; regulatory risk (GDPR/PIPA)

P1_HIGH_PRIORITY.issue: JSON body cast as { message: string } without Zod/type guard. If message is object/array, type system lies.
P1_HIGH_PRIORITY.code_snippet: const { message } = await req.json() as { message: string };
P1_HIGH_PRIORITY.fix: Use Zod: const schema = z.object({ message: z.string().min(10) }); const { message } = schema.parse(await req.json());
P1_HIGH_PRIORITY.impact: Type errors: message could be object; length check fails silently

P1_HIGH_PRIORITY.issue: calcTrend uses Number() on Decimal result. If saleAmount is very large (>2^53-1), precision lost.
P1_HIGH_PRIORITY.fix: Use Decimal.js or BigInt for currency. If must use Number, document max supported amount.
P1_HIGH_PRIORITY.impact: Financial data loss: trend calculations silently wrong for large amounts

P1_HIGH_PRIORITY.issue: getOrgSmsConfig() at line 134 has no null check before use. If getOrgSmsConfig returns Promise<null>, config.aligoKey will throw at line 139.
P1_HIGH_PRIORITY.fix: Correct — null check IS present. (Non-issue, but verify getOrgSmsConfig() always returns defined shape.)
P1_HIGH_PRIORITY.impact: None — false alarm. Config is properly validated.

P2_MEDIUM_PRIORITY.issue: calcTrend defined once (line 99) but identical logic repeated. Should be extracted.
P2_MEDIUM_PRIORITY.code_snippet:
const calcTrend = (curr: number, prev: number) =>
  prev === 0 ? (curr > 0 ? 100 : 0) : Math.round(((curr - prev) / prev) * 100);
P2_MEDIUM_PRIORITY.fix: Extract to utility lib/utils.ts as calculateTrendPercentage() for reuse across routes.
P2_MEDIUM_PRIORITY.impact: Maintainability: 4+ files define same calc; drift risk if changed

P2_MEDIUM_PRIORITY.issue: limit = 30 hardcoded. No const. Changed in multiple routes.
page > 10000 allowed. If user sets page=999999, skip overshoots. No max result set guard.
P2_MEDIUM_PRIORITY.fix: Keep page <= 10000. But also add: if (skip > ABSOLUTE_MAX_OFFSET) { cap at last page }.
P2_MEDIUM_PRIORITY.impact: DoS-lite: user can request huge offsets; DB scan of entire result set

P2_MEDIUM_PRIORITY.issue: BATCH_SIZE=10 and MAX_RECIPIENTS=200. Hard to tune for optimization.
P2_MEDIUM_PRIORITY.impact: Operations: can't tune without code change; production tuning inflexible

P2_MEDIUM_PRIORITY.issue: Role check (FREE_SALES + AGENT blocked) but OWNER+GLOBAL_ADMIN not explicitly approved. If new role added, default allow.
P2_MEDIUM_PRIORITY.impact: Security: new roles auto-inherit permission; implicit logic is fragile

P2_MEDIUM_PRIORITY.issue: monthParam.split('-').map(Number) can fail silently if format is invalid (e.g., '2026-13' or '2026-00').
P2_MEDIUM_PRIORITY.fix: Validate: if (!/^\d{4}-\d{2}$/.test(monthParam)) { throw ... }; or use date-fns parseISO().
P2_MEDIUM_PRIORITY.impact: UX: invalid month accepted; generates wrong date boundaries

immediate_actions: Wrap b2c auto-sync in $transaction() to prevent race condition (P0-001)
Add rate limit to suspension-status POST (P0-003)
Validate category parameter in image-sync endpoint (P0-007)
Fix SmsOptOut query to include organizationId filter (P0-005)
short_term_fixes: Replace b2b/detail raw SQL with Prisma queries or add SQL parameter validation (P0-002)
Audit and clarify GLOBAL_ADMIN org-scope bypass across all endpoints (P0-004)
Fix rate limit key fallback to throw on missing userId (P1-004)
Add Zod validation to all req.json() calls (P1-008)
refactoring: Extract calcTrend to lib/utils.ts for reuse (P2-001)
Whitelist roles explicitly instead of blacklist pattern (P2-005)
Create shared error handler middleware for consistent logging (P1-003)

production_readiness.assessment: NOT PRODUCTION-READY. Multiple critical security issues (IDOR, race conditions, injection risks) must be fixed before deployment. Rate limiting gaps and cross-org data leaks are unacceptable for multi-tenant system.
